steps.title: Find the handoff temperature
steps.content: This is a two-phase linear model. First find the temperature at the moment the rate changes. The liquid starts at $23^\circ\text{C}$ and warms at $6.5^\circ\text{C}$ per minute for $4.0$ minutes, so the increase is $6.5(4.0)=26$. The handoff temperature is $23+26=49^\circ\text{C}$.

steps.title: Build the second-phase line in direct
steps.content: After $4.0$ minutes, the new rate is $2.4^\circ\text{C}$ per minute. Use a tiny table starting at the handoff point: at $x=4$, $y=49$; one minute later, at $x=5$, $y=51.4$. A linear fit confirms the second phase is the line with slope $2.4$ through $(4,49)$.

steps.title: Match the point-slope form
steps.content: A line with rate $2.4$ starting from $49$ at time $4.0$ is $$y=2.4(x-4.0)+49.$$ The problem says the final temperature is $63^\circ\text{C}$, so set $y=63$: $$63=2.4(x-4.0)+49.$$ That matches choice C.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: Choice A uses $26$, which is only the first-phase increase, not the temperature at $4.0$ minutes. Choice B ignores the rate change. Choice D starts the second-phase line at $x=0$ instead of $x=4.0$. Choice C is correct.
